name: Mrs. Elizabeth Ovenstad's Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the boiling water over the whole-wheat kernels (or wheat berries) and let stand for an hour or two to soften.
2. Proof the yeast: Combine warm water and sugar in a bowl. Add active dry yeast and let sit until foamy, about 10 minutes.
3. In another bowl, combine rye flour, whole-meal flour, white flour, salt, drained wheat kernels, proofed yeast mixture, and an additional cup of warm water (or the water used to soak the wheat berries). Knead well, adding more white flour as needed until a smooth dough forms.
4. Form the dough into a ball, place in a greased bowl, turn to coat, and cover. Allow to rise until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
5. Punch down the dough and knead for another 10 minutes. Divide into two equal pieces, shape into loaves, and let rise again.
6. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for about 1 hour or until golden brown and hollow-sounding when tapped. The bread should have a good crunchy crust.
